title: Smoky Chewy Canadian Kabanos Stew
description: A hearty, slowcooked stew that showcases smoky, chewy vegetarian kabanos with earthy root vegetables and a mapleinfused broth, perfect for a comforting Canadian winter meal.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add diced onion and minced garlic; sauté until onions are translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in sliced carrots, celery, and potatoes; cook for another 3 minutes, letting the vegetables lightly brown.
Sprinkle in smoked paprika, salt, and pepper; stir to coat the vegetables evenly.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Add the sliced vegetarian kabanos and fresh thyme sprigs; simmer uncovered for an additional 10 minutes, letting the sausages absorb the broth.
Stir in maple syrup for a subtle sweetness that balances the smokiness; adjust seasoning as needed.
Remove the thyme sprigs, ladle the stew into bowls, and garnish with optional crumbled vegan cheese if desired.
Serve hot with crusty rye bread on the side.
